The pair's road trip takes an awkward and dangerous turn when they find that an inept federal agent (Tom Arnold) and the mobster (Bradley Cooper) he testified against are both trying to hunt them down.

programme.title: Blind Horizon [Broadcast Edit]
programme.desc: Found in the New Mexico desert with a head wound and total amnesia, Frank Kavanaugh (Val Kilmer) is brought to a hospital. When he recovers some of his memory, he begins raving about an upcoming assassination attempt on the president. Despite the man's unstable state, Sheriff Jack Kolb (Sam Shepard) is troubled by Frank's story. Discovering the truth grows more complicated when Chloe Richards (Neve Campbell) arrives and claims to be Frank's fiancée, even though he has no memory of her.

programme.title: Are You Here

programme.title: My Dinner With Andre

programme.desc: Brandon Ma is an average Joe but his best friend, Michael, is a sinister drug dealer with a crush on Brandon's girlfriend, May. When Michael murders a dirty cop and frames his friend, sending him to jail, Brandon returns for vengeance.

programme.title: Battle Creek Brawl

programme.title: Sophie's Choice
programme.desc: Adapted from William Styron's best-selling novel, this passionate tale of a writer's love for a holocaust survivor is an exhilarating and compelling revelation. Screen favorite Meryl Streep received an Academy Award´ for her portrayal of Sophie Zawistowska in this penetrating drama set in post-World War II Brooklyn. Kevin Kline plays her all-consuming lover, Nathan.

programme.title: Rambo: Last Blood

programme.title: Salvation
programme.desc: A foster child bonds with a snake-handling Pentecostal community but uncovers sinister truths. She must choose between reporting pastor's crimes or preserving her newfound support system, risking personal safety for a sense of belonging.

programme.title: 12 To Midnight
programme.desc: A detective gets kicked off the force when the murder of his wife drives him to drink. When a new string of murders seem connected to her death, he soon finds himself squaring off against a killer who appears to be superhuman.

programme.desc: Stacey survives a childhood of abuse and fear, only to face a shocking twist.

programme.desc: Impresario aids fiancée in search for her fallen climber, masking his true goal: finding a spy satellite.

programme.title: Let Love Grow
programme.desc: Landscape architect Evie meets her match with new client Ryan, whose daughters are her biggest fan.

programme.title: Holiday High School Reunion
programme.desc: Returning home for Christmas, a woman develops an unexpected attraction to her best friend from school.

programme.title: Home For The Holidays
programme.desc: A loving aunt struggles to adopt her young relatives after their parents die in a car accident.

programme.title: Hometown Holiday
programme.desc: Krista is starting the New Year with a new business and a resolution to be much more selective with who she dates. When Krista meets Ryan Rourke, an entertainment lawyer from LA, the two really hit it off.

programme.title: Just Getting Started
programme.desc: Duke Diver is living the high life as the freewheeling manager of a luxurious resort in Palm Springs, Calif. He soon faces competition from Leo, a former military man who likes the same woman that Duke is interested in. When Diver's past suddenly catches up with him, he must put aside his differences and reluctantly team up with Leo to stop whoever is trying to kill him.

programme.title: American Honey
programme.desc: Star (Sasha Lane), an adolescent girl from a troubled home, runs away with a traveling sales crew that drives across the American Midwest selling subscriptions door-to-door. Finding her feet in this gang of teenagers, one of whom is Jake (Shia LaBeouf), she soon gets into the group's lifestyle of hard-partying nights, law-bending days and young love.

programme.title: On Angel's Wings
programme.desc: When an angel grants a struggling water girl (Taylor Faye Ruffin) three wishes, even her mom (Robin Givens) or coach (Reginald VelJohnson) can't figure out how she became a soccer superstar.

programme.title: Take Back
programme.desc: Zara (Gillian White) and Brian (Michael Jai White) are living the perfect small town life when a mysterious stranger from Zara's secret past kidnaps their daughter and now they must race to save her life.

programme.title: Love Kills
programme.desc: In the heart of Atlanta's unpredictable dating scene, Darron is a hopeless romantic with the worst luck imaginable. Every time he believes he's found "the one," he ends up caught in another tangled web of passion, betrayal, and danger. When Darron finally meets someone who seems genuine, his past mistakes come back with a vengeance. One of his exes' jealous boyfriends discovers their hidden affair, setting off a chain of events that spirals out of control.

programme.title: Don't Miss the Bus
programme.desc: Sierra, a single mother hustles in order to survive with long shifts and grunt work. Her faith is pinned on lottery tickets and men who exploit her vulnerability. When a stroke of luck has the ability to change her future forever, a missed bus stop could mean life or death.

programme.title: Transcend
programme.desc: Maranda spent her life looking for a protector, only to find herself lost in a cycle of toxic love and betrayal. From the wreckage of abandonment to a life-altering crossroads, her survival isn't just about escaping the past, it’s about surviving herself.

programme.title: The Loyalty Test
programme.desc: Trey Harrison is an up-and-coming internet star that has created a series exposing cheaters in orchestrated sting operations, successfully turning heartbreak into a viral spectacle. But when two participants are found murdered, Trey’s world begins to crumble and things only begin to escalate.

programme.title: Murder on the Pole
programme.desc: One night Bunny was the queen of the dance floor; the next, she was a chalk outline. Dismissed by a department eager to sweep a "club girl's" death under the rug, Lola refuses to let her sister’s killer walk free. To find the murderer, Lola must infiltrate the world that claimed Bunny’s life—a world of predatory regulars, backstage betrayals, and a cover-up that goes higher than she ever imagined.

programme.title: Concealment 2: Open Marriage
programme.desc: Deandre & Nia, betrayed by each other & blackmailed by their college friend Isis, reluctantly open their marriage. Isis steals their money & disappears. Deandre seeks help to recover his losses. Both are involved in secret affairs, concealing crucial information from their partners, leading to tragic consequences.

programme.title: Battledogs
programme.desc: The Lupine virus turns Manhattan civilians into wolves. Unless a cure is found, only a nuke can stop it.

programme.title: Road Wars: Max Fury
programme.desc: In a post-apocalyptic wasteland, sisters try to save their mother armed only with a muscle car and ammo.

programme.title: End Of The World
programme.desc: As a mass of solar storms causes tsunamis, volcanoes, and flooding, a family flees to high-elevation caves.

programme.title: Z Nation
programme.desc: Mysterious things are starting to happen as our Heroes drive through RoZwell, New Mexico.

programme.title: Disaster Strike Force
programme.desc: The entire California coastline is threatened as a 20.0 earthquake looms.

programme.title: 6 Guns
programme.desc: After a woman’s family is slaughtered, she hires a gunslinger to teach her how to shoot to take vengeance.

programme.title: Thankskilling (Edited Version)
programme.desc: A homicidal turkey axes off college kids during Thanksgiving break.

programme.title: Stake Land (Edited Version)
programme.desc: America is a lost nation. When an epidemic of vampirism strikes and his family is slaughtered, young Martin (Gossip Girls Connor Paolo) is taken under the wing of a grizzled, wayward hunter (In the Cuts Nick Damici) whose new prey are the undead. Simply known as Mister, the vampire stalker takes Martin on a journey through the locked-down towns of Americas heartland, searching for a better place to call home while taking down any bloodsuckers that cross their path.

programme.title: Homebound (Edited Version)
programme.desc: HOMEBOUND is the feature debut and psychological thriller from director Sebastian Godwin, about a father (Tom Goodman-Hill) and his new fiance (Aisling Loftus) whose trip to the countryside, turns frightening when they arrive to find his ex-wife is missing and the kids acting strange.

programme.title: Mega Piranha (Edited Version)
programme.desc: A mutant strain of giant ferocious piranha escape from the Amazon and eat their way toward Florida.

programme.title: The Funhouse Massacre (Edited Version)
programme.desc: Six of the world's scariest psychopaths escape from a local Asylum and proceed to unleash terror on the unsuspecting crowd of a Halloween Funhouse whose themed mazes are inspired by their various reigns of terror.

programme.title: Horror's Greatest
programme.desc: What special film would you recommend to the ghoul who has "seen everything?" In this episode, genre mavens select 13 movies that have been forgotten, overlooked, unfairly maligned, or are hard to find - 13 bloody gems hidden beneath the floorboards.

programme.title: Horror's Greatest
programme.desc: From King Kong's epic orchestra to the unnerving electronics of Under the Skin, composers have used every style of music to frighten audiences. Horror scores blaze trails, bringing new instruments and radical musical forms into popular consciousness.

programme.title: Horror's Greatest
programme.desc: Alien. 2001. Forbidden Planet. Many of the greatest sci-fi movies ever made are also horror films. The vastness of space, the claustrophobia of space travel, and the inconceivable horrors that might lurk in the void make for spine-tingling filmmaking

programme.title: Clovehitch Killer, The
programme.desc: A picture-perfect family is shattered when the work of a serial killer hits too close to home. Dylan McDermott stars in this chilling portrait of all-American evil.

programme.title: Reef, The
programme.desc: Four friends are left stranded and must swim through shark-infested waters to shore. A Great White immediately follows them for a white-knuckle chase that will fill the waters with blood!

programme.title: Banquet, A
programme.desc: A mother is radically tested when her teenage daughter insists a supernatural experience has left her body in service to a higher power.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Family Tree" - Mrs. Drysdale wants to get rid of the Clampetts before the arrival of Mrs. Smith-Standish, the head of a 'first family' historical society. But Mrs. Smith-Standish goes to the Clampetts' first and becomes enchanted with their antiques and way of life, even to roping the horrified Mrs. Drysdale into helping do the chores the old-fashioned way.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed Becomes a Banker" - Drysdale hires Jed to be a banker when he needs a crack shot to help beat a rival banker in a skeet shooting competition, but his partner needs to be an employee.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed Buys the Freeway" - Henry Jones shows up at the Clampett's, pretends to be an old friend and tries to sell the family the Hollywood Bowl, Griffith Park, and the freeway.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Duke Steals a Wife" - Jed and Duke his dog are both down without a woman in their lives, until they see and pursue a French woman and her poodle that she brought to breed with Mrs. Drysdales dog. Meanwhile Mrs. Drysdale has arranged a "marriage" between the dogs, complete with a decorated bedroom.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed Plays Solomon" - Granny can't stand Pearl's yodeling so she reports her to the police. When one of the officers takes Pearl out, Granny thinks she got Pearl in trouble and becomes sad and contrite. Meanwhile, the police find her still, and new trouble results.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed Throws a Wingding" - Flatt and Scruggs and their wives come to visit their old sweetheart, but Pearl thinks they're there to propose. Jed throws a wingding for them and the duo play music for the Clampetts.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Elly's Animals" - When Pearl starts selling music lessons, Mrs. Drysdale complains about the noise to the police. Mrs. Drysdale also calls the dog catcher on Duke.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies

Jed didn't count on Mr. Brewster's background in the theater and him making a huge production out of it.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Pygmalion and Elly" - Sonny Drysdale decides he needs to be Pygmalion to Elly's Galatea and remake her from a hillbilly into a woman of society, while Granny uses love charms to heat up their relationship.

programme.title: Yellowneck
programme.desc: Location shooting in the Florida Everglades add to this dramatic tale of five Confederate deserters trying to get to Cuba. First, they must overcome snake bites, unfriendly Seminoles, and their own conflicts with each other. How many of the five will actually make it out alive? Lin McCarthy, Stephen Courtleigh, and Berry Kroeger star in this little known example of 1950s independent filmmaking.

programme.title: The Lucky Texan
programme.desc: John Wayne stars in this vintage western. He and George Gabby Hayes strike it rich with a gold mine, only to find their lives complicated by women and crooked officials. Things get even messier when the good guys are framed for murder! Its all action here, particularly since legendary stuntman Yakima Cannut stars as one of the films heavies. Who will win the fight over the gold and the girl? Directed by Robert N. Bradbury for Monogram Pictures. Also starring Barbara Sheldon. (The film was also known as Gold Strike River.)

programme.title: Wagon Trail
programme.desc: The son of Sheriff Clay Hartley, of the frontier town Elder, has gotten into bad company and hangs out with an outlaw gang in which, Collins, owner of the Golden Rule Saloon, is the secret head. Sheriff Hartley suspects him, but has been unable to gather the needed evidence. Collins instructs his gang, including young Hartley, to hold up the stagecoach on its return trip from Missionary Flats and take the cargo of gold dust it is carrying. Sheriff Hartley is notified of the planned holdup by one of his deputies who has been spying on Collins, and organizes a posse. A deputy-sheriff is killed in the ensuing gunfight between the lawmen and the outlaws, but Deputy Joe Larkin, pursues and captures Clay Hartley Jr. The latter is quickly tried and convicted of the killing of the deputy, and sentenced to be hung. Sheriff Hartley has only a few hours to prove his son was not the killer. He enlists the aid of Collins' step-daughter, Joan, who is in love with Hartley's son.

programme.title: One-Eyed Jacks
programme.desc: Dad Longworth robs a Mexican bank, takes the loot, and leaves his partner Rio to be captured. However, Rio escapes capture and searches for Dad in California.

programme.title: The Deadly Companions
programme.desc: An unholy alliance! Marking the feature film debut of one of Hollywoods most influential and widely referenced auteur directors, Sam Peckinpah, The Deadly Companions centers on a redemptive journey to escort a funeral procession through dangerous Indian Territory. Featuring fine work from a top-notch cast that includes the inimitable Maureen OHara at the height of her illustrious career, Peckinpahs first foray into film provides a promising glance into the energetic and violent nature of his iconic later works.

programme.title: Last of the Clintons
programme.desc: Icon of early talking pictures, Harry Carey (Red River), portrays the rugged Trigger Carson, a noble cowboy who infiltrates an outlaw gang in order to expose their rackets. Ordered to kidnap a young girl, his identity is revealed and he is forced to fight his way out of the gang and back to safety. Already 56 by the time he signed on to do this and a series of Westerns for Berke Productions, the stately Carrey (who netted an Oscar nomination four years later) was widely lauded at the time for adding a great deal of maturity and respectability due to his undeniable presence and strong performances.

Andrew's latest special is an absolute laugh riot, packed with relatable chaos from airport adventures to quirky family dynamics. Brace yourself for non-stop hilarity from start to finish!

programme.title: The Crush
programme.desc: An obsessive teenage crush.

programme.title: The Nut Job
programme.desc: An incorrigibly self-serving exiled squirrel finds himself helping his former park brethren survive by raiding a nut store, a location that also happens to be a front for a human gang's bank robbery.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Celebrity chef Rocco DiSpirito joins the Chewed Up crew for a very special Christmas Eve episode as Carla Hall prepares elegant Blue Crab Spaghetti. The conversation covers air fryer controversies, Christmas Eve traditions, and the art of making a restaurant-quality seafood pasta at home.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Carla Hall leads Christmas morning with her beloved Cinnamon Rolls featuring a cream cheese frosting brightened with orange zest, sharing her make-ahead technique that lets the rolls be prepared days in advance and baked fresh on Christmas morning. The hosts discuss holiday baking rituals and the joy of waking up to something already prepared.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: The Chewed Up crew rings in New Year's Eve week with a cocktail-forward episode featuring Clinton Kelly mixing festive vodka-based drinks while the hosts reflect on the year ending and new beginnings ahead. Sparkles makes her return, adding her signature energy to the holiday finale.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Michael Symon celebrates New Year's Eve by sharing his Eastern European family tradition of eating pork and sauerkraut for good luck, making a simple and deeply satisfying Pork and Sauerkraut Stew with pork shoulder, smoked paprika, and beer. The hosts stay in low-key holiday mode, swapping stories about card games, New Year's traditions, and the comfort of staying home.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Carla Hall rings in the New Year by cooking Black-Eyed Pea Stew with collard greens and rice, a Southern tradition she considers essential for bringing luck, health, and prosperity into the new year. Special guest Deb Riegel, an executive coach and fifth-generation New Yorker, joins to discuss the difference between New Year's resolutions and intentions.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Renowned chef Charlie Palmer joins Clinton Kelly, Carla Hall, and Michael Symon for a fascinating conversation about American cuisine and farm-to-table sourcing, while demonstrating his elegant Paccheri with Koginut Squash PurÈe and Stracciatella. Palmer shares the origin story of his annual Pigs and Pinot event and his philosophy of building relationships with farmers to source specialty ingredients.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: The Chewed Up Show dives into world-class dips with hosts Michael Symon and Clinton Kelly, joined by guest Ayesha Nurdjaja. Clinton prepares a simple homemade tzatziki with Greek yogurt, cucumber, garlic, and fresh herbs, discussing how it's become a healthy staple in his household. Carla brings a grilled corn dip featuring mayo, sour cream, scallions, cilantro, and charred corn. Michael shares a Cleveland clam dip recipe—a regional specialty featuring diced clams, bacon, dill, and sour cream. The hosts discuss dip culture, texture preferences, and creative variations like using kohlrabi and roasted beets. Between recipes, they play never-have-I-ever games and answer fan questions about dessert preferences and vacation spots.

programme.title: Simply Symons
programme.desc: Michael Symon, Liz Symon, and Liv Annacone welcome their Venice neighbor Rose Previte, chef and owner of the acclaimed Michelin-starred restaurant Maidan, to craft beef souvlaki kebabs with grilled pita and tzatziki. Rose shares her culinary journey and the spices that define Middle Eastern and Greek kebab traditions—from sumac and fresh mint to warming spices like cinnamon and Syrian seven-spice blend. The episode also features a wine pairing with rare Georgian amber wines from two female winemakers, including the endangered Del Shavi grape variety. Throughout, the hosts discuss Rose's innovative Maidan Market concept in Los Angeles and their shared Midwestern roots.

programme.title: Simply Symons
programme.desc: On Simply Symons, Michael Symon, his wife Liz, and Liv Annacone cook Big Rigs rigatoni with spicy Italian sausage and vodka sauce in their front yard. Michael demonstrates his "cheater sauce" approach using pre-seasoned sausage, tomato paste, cream, and vodka that comes together in under 30 minutes without needing a knife. The hosts discuss pasta cooking techniques, the importance of finishing pasta in sauce rather than plating separately, proper seasoning of pasta water, and using quality ingredients like whole Parmigiano-Reggiano. They pair the dish with Lambrusco, an Italian sparkling red wine, exploring its versatility with various cuisines and its ability to cut through rich, spicy flavors.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Michael Symon elevates the humble chicken tender by using a feta brine to infuse the meat with tangy, salty flavor before breading and shallow-frying to golden perfection. He finishes the dish with a bold hot honey sauce made with Calabrian chili, creating an indulgent yet sophisticated take on a crowd-pleasing classic.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Carla Hall tackles a Fan Fridge from a viewer in Ohio, transforming ground beef, mashed potatoes, and pantry staples into a warming Shepherd's Pie-inspired casserole layered with sautÈed vegetables and finished with a cheesy potato topping. The hosts weave in a thoughtful conversation about grace, kindness to strangers, and the community spirit of how neighborhoods once raised children together.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly, Carla Hall, and Michael Symon each take a third of the same chocolate sheet cake and decorate it their own way, creating three wildly different showstopping desserts in one episode. Clinton goes peanut butter, Carla goes Black Forest-inspired with sour cherry, and Michael goes coconut ganache ó proving that one bake can launch a thousand creative directions.

programme.title: Behind the Counter with David Chang
programme.desc: Behind the Counter with David Chang - Classic Comforts: An unfiltered behind-the-scenes look into David Chang's kitchen as he and his team develop and practice a unique menu in the days leading up to the next Netflix episode of Dinner Time Live. This episode sees Dave working on a "Classic Comfort Foods" menu for NBA legends Reggie Miller and Mark Jackson. Dishes include: Caesar salad, New England clam chowder, fettuccine alfredo, salmon with caper-butter sauce, a potato rösti, and a chocolate tart.

programme.title: Behind the Counter with David Chang
programme.desc: Behind the Counter with David Chang - Choose Your Own Adventure Menu: An unfiltered behind-the-scenes look into David Chang's kitchen as he and his team develop and practice a unique menu in the days leading up to the next Netflix episode of Dinner Time Live. This episode sees Dave working on a "Classic Comfort Foods" menu for NBA legends Reggie Miller and Mark Jackson. Dishes include: Caesar salad, New England clam chowder, fettuccine alfredo, salmon with caper-butter sauce, a potato rösti, and a chocolate tart.

programme.title: Behind the Counter with David Chang
programme.desc: Behind the Counter with David Chang - Fish on the Menu: An unfiltered behind-the-scenes look into David Chang's kitchen as he and his team develop and practice a unique menu in the days leading up to the next Netflix episode of Dinner Time Live. In this episode, Dave is creating a "Fish on the Menu" theme for Emily in Paris stars Lily Collins and Ashley Park. Dishes include: Hwe (two ways), Uni & Chickpeas, an Ode to Noma's Langoustine, Roasted Flounder, Fish Soup with Rice and Pickles, and Flottante.

programme.title: Ying Cooks the Internet
programme.desc: "Chris Ying's Family Curry Recipe with Twice-Fried Potatoes, Chicken, and Roux-Thickened Coconut Milk" - It's Chris Ying's favorite dish on earth. His last-request meal. It's his dad's curry: a version of Macanese (as in, from Macao) Chicken: a roux-thickened coconut curry full of twice-fried potatoes and fried chicken thighs, flavored with ginger and Shaoxing wine. It's Chris's comfort food, which is fitting, since he's feeling a little emotionally battered as he makes it for us. Will the curry do its magic and pick him up? Only one way to find out!

programme.title: Ying Cooks the Internet
programme.desc: "Mango Pie with California Mangoes and Graham Cracker Crust" - Chris Ying finds himself with four really expensive, fancy mangoes, so he tries out a viral mango-pie recipe from Samin Nosrat and Hrishikesh Hirway.

programme.title: Ying Cooks the Internet
programme.desc: "SPOOOOKY Halloween Treats!" - Chris cracks into his wife's Pinterest account to see what sort of Halloween recipes she's interested in, and is horrified by what he finds: Crescent Roll Mummy Dogs and Cheesy Halloween Breadstick Bones. We decided to throw in Witch Finger Pretzels to BOO-t. (sigh) These may not be the most culinarily advanced recipes you'll see Chris experiment with, but they are genuinely fun holiday treats that would be fun to do with your little monsters.

programme.title: Ying Cooks the Internet
programme.desc: "Pizza Lover's Pizza" - Inspired by the stunt pies of the internet, Chris Ying is bringing his own dream pizza to life: the Bagel-Bite-topped Boboli. Also featured in this episode: a 19th century Italian love saga. Boboli and Bagel Bites—let’s collab.

programme.title: Ying Cooks the Internet
programme.desc: "Silky Spicy Eggplant" - Chris is joined by beloved cookbook author Hetty McKinnon to test out “EASY & QUICK VEGAN EGGLANT RECIPE IN CHINA” from TikTok/IG sensation @foodiechina888 (a.k.a. Wayne Shen).

programme.title: Ying Cooks the Internet
programme.desc: "The ONE Pancake" - Chris tackles the fourth most Googled recipe of 2022: "Quick Pancake." His solution? THE ONE PANCAKE. Every ingredient is reduced to a unit of one (1 cup or 1 teaspoon), hence, THE ONE. It's literally an unforgettable recipe for delicious, and, most importantly, quick pancakes.

programme.title: Cooking School
programme.desc: "David Chang Makes an Epic Thanksgiving Leftover Sandwich" - Dave has joined forces with Amazon Prime Thursday Night Football to create a new tradition for their first-ever Black Friday Football game on November 24—make a ridiculous sandwich for the game MVP using Thanksgiving leftovers. This is Dave's first attempt, so there are plenty of learnings discovered along the way as he tries to construct a worthy sandwich...making an entire Thanksgiving spread along the way. Get ready to unhinge your jaw, because this sandwich is something straight out of Shaggy and Scoob's playbook.

programme.title: Cooking School
programme.desc: "Lisa Donovan's Legendary Pie Crust" - There are pie-makers, and then there's world-renowned pastry chef Lisa Donovan. Lisa blesses the MajordomoTV Studio by taking us step-by-step through her zen-like process of making a perfect, flaky, tender, Southern-style pie crust. The technique! The poetry! The jokes about oversized rolling pins! Lisa is a true master of the pie arts, and if you've ever had ambitions to make your own crust—or just like to zone out watching hands sifting through butter and flour—there's no better place to start than a lesson from Ms. Donovan.

programme.title: Cooking School
programme.desc: "David Chang recreates his mom's holiday specialty (bindaetteok 빈대떡)" - David Chang recreates his mom's holiday specialty (bindaetteok 빈대떡) - When Dave Chang was growing up, his mom always made mung-bean pancakes with kimchi, scallion, and pork belly for any celebratory dinner. So, as Dave gears up for the holidays, he's trying to channel her culinary energy into his own version of Korean bindaetteok. Try as he might, he just can't get it to taste exactly the same, but isn't that what makes moms special?

programme.title: Cooking School
programme.desc: "Chicken Pot Pie from scratch in 45 minutes?! REAL-TIME COOKING" - After a long day of filming in the studio kitchen, Dave Chang wanted one more challenge. For no reason other than to see if he could do it, he attempted the utterly ridiculous task of making chicken pot pie, completely from scratch, in under 45 minutes. This is no BS! Dave enlists his buddy, Chris Ying, to help. The guys have nothing prepped, no plan, and no recipes. Dave’s gonna tackle the crust. Chris is gonna make the filling. The timer starts, and they’re off. WILL THEY MAKE IT HAPPEN?

programme.title: Cooking School
programme.desc: "Dave Chang makes BEER CAN CHICKEN the wrong way (and it’s so good)" - Two days after failing at his first ever Beer Can Chicken attempt, Dave gets right back on the horse. He’s committed to getting it right, keeping it simple, and making the white meat as delicious as the dark. Plus: he demonstrates a lazy man's beer pan sauce that will make you question ever doing things the “right” way again.

programme.title: Cooking School
programme.desc: "David Chang upgrades his Momofuku Sweet & Spicy Noodles (with a perfect fried egg!)" - Dave cooks up some of the Momofuku Sweet & Spicy Noodles and tricks them out with Korean dongchimi (pickled radish), kimchi and a—ahem—*perfectly* fried egg.

programme.title: Winning Mealtime
programme.desc: Winning Mealtime - Japanese Curry Your Kids (and You!) Will Love: Chris Ying shares his recipe for a creamy, dreamy Japanese curry that uses milk and honey to hit all the right notes that kids love. If your kids already like curry...you're golden with this recipe! And if your kids are new to the curry game, this is the perfect introduction.

programme.title: Emeril Cooks

programme.title: Billy & Dom Eat The World
programme.desc: Billy and Dom share their favorite haunts in Los Angeles, including the famous Mel’s Diner in Santa Monica. They take a trip to Sonoma County for a farm-to-table adventure and get a rare opportunity to take a fascinating glimpse at the world’s most advanced lab-grown meat facility in Oakland.

programme.title: Billy & Dom Eat The World
programme.desc: Billy and Dom traverse the Isles of Lewis and Harris, where they attend an agricultural show and are invited to a traditional Scottish cèilidh. They meet the Hebridean Baker who treats them to an alfresco dinner on the Isle of Taransay, and visit the Isle of Harris Distillery to try their offering of gin and whisky.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: Diane embraces the words of Hippocrates, "Let food be thy medicine and medicine be thy food." She explores Greek staples—olive oil, herbs, nuts, veggies—as health foundations. Plus, three simple recipes and a chat with Dr. William Li, author of Eat to Beat Disease.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: How do you take a meat-centric plate and shift things around so that vegetables take up the prominent place and meat plays a secondary role? Diane and her son Yiorgos explore the geography of the dinner plate by recreating classic Greek recipes for meat with, well, less of it!

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: Diane shares how shopping for, cleaning and cooking greens is easy and accessible. She visits her 96-year-old cousin’s garden, this vibrant produce may hold the key to her longevity. In the kitchen, Diane prepares a green storm and sips a surprising elixir from cooked greens.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: Inspired by the ancient Greek philosopher and mathematician, Pythagoras, Diane explores plant-based cooking rooted in his ancient vegetarian philosophy. She discusses modern nutrition’s alignment with Pythagorean principles and chats with Prof. John Wilkins on Ancient Greek foods and medicines.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: Diane journeys from Greek forests to an Athenian restaurant exploring the health benefits of mushrooms. Plus, she chats with Dr. Andrew Weil on mushroom research and longevity, then cooks a mushroom gyro and braised mushroom-chickpea dish. She also tastes a rare recipe by Chef Gikas Xenakis.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: In the traditional Greek and Mediterranean diet, seasonal dishes feature naturally probiotic ingredients such as pickled vegetables, olives and yogurt. Diane explores these probiotic foods and shares ways to incorporate them into everyday recipes. Over tea, she discusses the benefits of probiotic foods with Dr. Theoklis Zaoutis.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: Beans are a superfood linked to longevity and play a starring role in Greek cuisine. Diane explores dried vs. canned beans, visits an Athens legume shop, and chats with Dr. William Li on their health benefits. She shares satisfying, easy-to-make recipes to help you eat well and feel great.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: Fasting is deeply rooted in Greek culture and religion. Diane explores some of the plant-based foods of abstention that make up part of the fasting diet and speaks with Professor Valter Longo, whose research on the benefits of fasting for longevity and wellness have catapulted him to global renown.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Smoke
programme.desc: In this "surf and turf" show, a marine biologist brings ocean-fresh sea urchins for Steven to grill. Local spiny lobster is split and roasted in the shell directly on the embers. Meanwhile, Alaskan king crab gets grilled and served with garlicky absinthe butter. Representing turf are bone-in veal chops blasted with rosemary smoke and beef plate ribs, spit-roasted on a wood-burning rotisserie.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Smoke
programme.desc: Steven takes a field trip to the famed Hitching Post II in Buellton, California, where owner Frank Ostini demonstrates the ultimate Santa Maria barbecue. Back on the set, Santa Barbara fisherwoman Stephanie Munz shares her secret for grilled whelks (sea snails). The celebration continues as we meet some of the production crew and go behind the scenes of Project Smoke.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Smoke
programme.desc: Victory will be yours! Start the party with chicken legs smoke-roasted over maple wood, then glazed with a fiery fusion of maple syrup and sriracha. A quartet of grilled and smoked beans appears in a colorful side dish. Next up, a trio of internationally-inspired burgers: inside-out American-style burgers; Greek-style spiced lamb burgers; and kaffir lime-scented Thai veal burgers.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Smoke
programme.desc: Its bright but complex flavors and affinity for smoke and fire have fueled the obsession with Mexican food and drink north of the border. Tomatoes, onions, and chiles are roasted in the coals to make an unforgettable salsa. Quesadillas conceal a perfectly cooked egg. Then, a Grilled Nopalitos Corn Salad and chipotle-marinated pork tenderloin. To finish, pineapple slices flambéed with mezcal.

programme.title: Extreme Weather Countdown
programme.desc: Are you ready to be taken on the ride of a lifetime as you journey through sandstorms, thunderstorms, and snow storms? I sure hope so, because that's what's in store here on another episode of Extreme Weather Countdown!

programme.title: Extreme Weather Countdown
programme.desc: Things heat up with sweltering heat waves, then cool down with icy blizzards on Extreme Weather Countdown!

programme.title: Extreme Weather Countdown

programme.title: Hellfire Heroes

programme.title: The Rocky Mountains

programme.title: Mountain Lakes, United States

programme.title: Summer Sunset Into Water
programme.desc: Unwind with a beautiful and picturesque New Jersey Shore sunset. Relax as the clouds and birds slowly drift across the sky and the magnificent sun lights the sky on fire.

programme.title: Mr. Monkey, Monkey Mechanic Collection 2
programme.desc: Join Mr. Monkey, Monkey Mechanic, as he repairs a whole new bunch of vehicles for even more animal friends!

programme.title: Weepy French Onion Soup - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Today, the Bumble Nums are going to make Weepy French Onion Soup. But the four weepy onions they need are VERY tricky to find. Will Humble, Grumble, and Stumble be able to spot all the onions and get back to the kitchen without crying too much? Watch and see!

programme.title: Put On Your Shoes - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Here’s a Super Simple Song all about getting dressed! Sing along as we put on our shoes, jacket, hat, and scarf and then head out for a walk with the family.

programme.title: With My Heart - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Feel the warmth and learn the five senses in this heart warming Super Simple sensory song for kids. It’s also a perfect song to celebrate all parents. I can hear with my ears. I can see with my eyes.

programme.title: Let's Count To 100 - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: We are learning to count to 100 with Finny the Shark! This is a perfect numbers song for young kids and preschoolers! Let’s count to 100. Counting’s so much fun. Let’s count to 100. We’ll start from number 1.

programme.title: Seven Steps - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Counting to seven has never been more memorable and fun! Learn numbers with this simple counting song.

programme.title: Goodbye A, Goodbye Z - Super Simple ABCs and more
programme.desc: We're saying, "Goodbye" to all of our friends in Alphabet Village. As the school day ends, take a walk through town, and bid farewell to all of the letters.

programme.title: I Like To Ride My Bicycle (Mr. Monkey) - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Let's go on a bike ride with Mr. Monkey and friends as we sing along to this Mr. Monkey version of the original Super Simple Song, "I Like To Ride My Bicycle." This is a great song to introduce during transportation themes! I like to ride my bicycle. I ride it every day. I ride it when I go to school. Or when I want to play.

programme.title: What's Your Name? - Kids Song - Finny the Shark and more
programme.desc: Finny the Shark is saying "Hello" and asking the question "What's your name?" This is a fun song to start the day at preschool! Hello, hello. What’s your name? Hello, hello. What’s your name? My name is Finny! My name is Myrtle! Nice to meet you.

programme.title: Wind The Bobbin Up - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: “Wind the bobbin up. Wind the bobbin up. Pull, pull, clap, clap, clap.” Get up and dance with this super fun version of the traditional English nursery rhyme.

programme.title: Hot Cross Buns - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Here’s one of our favorite traditional nursery rhymes about food, made super simple. Come sing along with the Chef from our “Do You Know The Muffin Man?” and “Pat-A-Cake” videos. Yum yum!

programme.title: Flying Dragon Fruit Cake - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Come forth brave warriors and watch the Bumble Nums catch some Flying Dragon Fruits for their fiery Flying Dragon Fruit Cake! But be careful! They breathe fire and are very fast!

programme.title: Carl's Big Adventure - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Carl is visited by three large, very dirty construction vehicles. But Carl’s Car Wash is not big enough to fit them! Carl has an idea! He can supersize his car wash so the vehicles can get a proper scrub-a-dub-dub. He just needs a few parts. With the help of old friends, Carl sets out on a big adventure to the darkest depths of the ocean, the highest heights in the land, and beyond! Don't miss Carl in his first mini-movie as he pushes himself to the limit in the name of vehicular cleanliness!

programme.title: Where Is Baby? - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Peekaboo! Sing along with this adorable song for kids, “Where Is Baby?” Where is baby? Where is baby? Where is baby? There he is.

programme.title: Finny's First Day Of School - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Finny is a bit nervous on the first day of school, but Myrtle the Turtle is even more anxious and literally hides her shell! Surely Myrtle will come out of her shell when their teacher, a whale named Mr. Magellan, has them draw and present their favorite things to the class! Nope. Myrtle is even more nervous than ever! Being a good friend, Finny helps Myrtle through her anxiety, and the tiny turtle finally shows her drawing to everyone.

programme.title: Baby Shark - Finny The Shark and more
programme.desc: Baby Shark, Mama Shark, Papa Shark, Grandma Shark, and Grandpa Shark are out for a family swim and Baby Shark tries and tries to catch the little fishies but his skills aren’t yet up to the task. Just when he thinks he finally has them a (very big) friend of the little fishies steps in to save the day!

programme.title: Dharma's Digger - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Oh no! While digging deep at a top secret construction site, Dhama’s digger gets splashed with smelly sludge! Everyone says that Carl's Car Wash is the place to get all cleaned up, so Dharma skedaddles over to see how Carl can help. With a quick mess assessment and a few twists of some knobs, Carl’s suds-sational car wash is ready to give Dharma’s vehicle a good scrub-a-dub! Dharma rolls out one happy customer, and ready to get back to work on the site that Carl is itching to see!

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Sharing the joys and traditions of the holiday season with your little one is truly one of the greatest gifts of parenthood, and Baby Santa's Music Box makes this magical time even more special! Celebrate by treating your baby and yourself to a captivating wonderland of bright lights, sparkling ornaments, sleigh bells, toys, winter scenes, jolly Santas and more -- all accompanied by beloved holiday melodies.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Sing along to your favorite classical melodies and enjoy engaging imagery in this space and sky themed episode. Young viewers can learn words like star and moon through repetition and narration, explore with the Baby Einstein characters, or simply dance along.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Journey to the classroom with your favorite Baby Einstein friends! Learn about what makes your face incredible, baby's first sounds, and counting in different languages with a focus on Spanish! Real-life engaging imagery, upbeat classical melodies and gentle narration and repetition introduce first words, faces and a variety of different languages to young viewers.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Perhaps the sweetest sounds you'll ever hear are the babbles and coos that one day become ma-ma, da-da and I love you. Besides being music to your ears, these baby steps of language change everything as your little one discovers an exciting, powerful new way of relating to the world! Baby's First Sounds invites both of you to share these memorable moments: those precious ''firsts'' that come around once in your baby's lifetime.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Within the first year of babies' lives, their physical development begins to kick into high gear as they explore ways to physically interact with the world around them. BABY'S FIRST MOVES is a joyful celebration of their movement milestones -- including first steps, clapping, jumping, dancing and more.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Share a rainbow of sights and sounds with your baby as you discover the wonders of color together! Join puppet host "Vincent Van Goat" as he creates six Van Gogh masterpieces exploring red, green, blue, yellow, orange, and purple. World Of Colors is a spirited celebration of creative expression.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Ocean Explorers
programme.desc: Cal, Neptune, Opus, Dean, Tinker and others learn about the flora and fauna of the ocean through STEAM-based principles and real-life footage.

programme.title: The Sandbox
programme.desc: The Sandbox is where our favorite Baby Einstein characters gather to play, problem-solve and have fun. Each five-minute episode explores an area of curiosity relevant to our 2-4 year old audience. Since the four weathered planks of wood that make up the structure of their sandbox are no obstacles for their imaginations, the entire universe is available to them for each new adventure.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As little ones become better acquainted with the world, they grow curious about animals of all kinds, beyond just the family pet. And as their communication skills continue to develop, they enjoy imitating animal sounds as well. Baby Noah Animal Expedition takes your little explorer across the globe to meet animals -- two-by-two -- from the savannah, the rainforest and tropics, the Outback, the ocean and the Polar Regions.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Dive in with your little explorer across the seas to meet amazing animals living in the ocean, coral reef, and polar regions. Overflowing with underwater footage of whales, dolphins, tropical fish, and more, Neptune's Oceans invites you and your baby on an oceanic voyage of discovery!

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Discover instruments through narration and repetition. Real-life images and sounds will engage viewers as they dance to upbeat classical favorites from Beethoven, Mozart and more. Join your favorite Baby Einstein characters to discover what makes each instrument unique!

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Baby Lullaby takes your baby on a soothing journey with classical lullabies by Bach, Brahms and Mozart. Enjoy relaxing ocean waves, sweet puppet shows and tranquil images. From fun time to rest time, Baby Lullaby is the perfect way to spend special time with your baby.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Babies love animals, and now you can bring animals from the seven continents home to your baby. Meet dozens of exotic animals including Asian pandas, Australian kangaroos and Arctic snow monkeys...all filmed in their beautiful, natural habitats. Rhythms and songs inspired by faraway lands have been orchestrated especially for little ears and encourage your baby to sing, dance and play to the world beats. You'll laugh together at the funny puppet shows featuring moms and their baby animals.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Super Space Scouts and Jett, Matthew, and Dizzy chase a giant ball through a black hole. They save Earth from a moon-sized ball and deliver it to Mars.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Chloe and Dad escape a pterosaur world with a special glider after a time slip. Jett swaps roles, causing chaos with an inflatable duck, and learns teamwork.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Super Wings take Kamal and Nadia to the dinosaur era for a sports showdown, revealing all dinosaurs' strengths. In Scotland, they fix an inflatable playground for exhausted Nessie.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett and Lucie clean Amara's dad's underwater base and help an injured shark. Julian's safety gear interview reveals a crane's trouble and a forklift's heroics.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett and Dizzy help Louis with a chaotic tennis match and AI machine, leading to his success. Jett and Lucie recover a lost golden record from an alien spacecraft, sparking joy.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: At Noah’s park, Camira hunts block dino eggs with Super Wings, facing surprises when Golden Girl snatches the prize and the egg escapes! Powered by new pod gear, Camira triumphs, while Jett braves the Sahara to find Latu and a fennec fox.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett and Dizzy jet to Jeju, helping Taehoon’s robot save Grandma’s shellfish from a giant fish, then team up to free it. On Mars, Jett and Marty chase Roy's lost bags, while Jinga dazzles with an out-of-this-world tour.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett and Dizzy join Hazel and Runi in Australia to help animals escape wildfires, then fly to Seoul where tiny Antny enters the Strongest Bug Contest, faces rampaging rivals, and, with Super Wings’ upgrades, helps save the city.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett and Marty help wash Maxy the giant plane and calm geysers at Oyunuma Pond, then deliver a Wright Flyer to Braden, sparking a time-hop to the Wright brothers, where teamwork lifts spirits and the Flyer takes flight.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett, Lucie, and Runi’s friendly contest becomes teamwork when they rescue toys from a Vietnam factory fire. Soon, Jett races to Dino World, freeing stranded friends and reuniting a T-Rex family as quakes rumble. Super Wings deliver!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Noah and the Dino Wings turn hail into fruit seeds, planting a thriving farm with Jett’s help and saving Dino World from a flood. Later, teamwork and a daring rescue inside Supersaurus bring the friends closer as everyone returns home safe.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: In Sicily, Jett and Curie foil invisibility pranks and stop a wild volcano eruption with Curie’s snow launcher, teaching Renata as chaos turns to learning. Aboard Ned’s ship, Super Electric Suits help them rescue everyone from a junk robot.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: While the World Spaceport celebrates Jimbo’s retirement, Jett faces a UFO and Space Monster, piloting World Robot as chaos erupts. The team unites, time freezes, and Jimbo reveals Megamon is an old friend, choosing to stay with everyone.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett and the Super Wings liven up Xiang Xiang’s Tanghulu shop, rescuing everyone from a syrupy mess and drawing big crowds. In Dino World, the Dino Wings halt a Dinobase takeover and reunite a wandering pterosaur hatchling with its mom.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Floods and thunder strike Dino World but Trico overcomes his fear to help Jett and the Dino Wings rescue every dino with paper boats and planes. Later Noah and Mom’s dino costume adventure leads to a mix-up with real maiasauras, and families reunite.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: In Paris, Jett and Curie shrink to save moles from a burst pipe during a wild croquet chase. Later, Jett and Sara help Princess Maribel when magical dress chaos erupts, restoring order so the party sparkles brighter than ever.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: On the moon, Jett and Curie outsmart dust storms and shield a spacecraft to help Kajsa ride her new bike. In Mexico’s Baja Desert, Jett and Marty clear boulders so Big Wheel can race again and roll to victory, powered by Super Wings spirit.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: Robotnik plans to block out the sun and extinguish all life and nature on the planet Mobius. Then he creates a robot son and tries to train him in the ways of wickedness.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Sonic conquers Robotnik's latest evil - a vehicle designated to locate and destroy living tissue matter - during a solo mission to the Badlands. Upon Sonic's return, the Freedom Fighters honor him with a victory party.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: When Robotnik launches a spy-orb that will uncover the Freedom Fighter's Knothole location, Sonic and Robot launch themselves up to the orbiting spy-sphere aboard a home-made rocket powered by Robotnik's discarded booster engine.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: When Sally uncovers Robotnik's plan to seed the cloud with toxic rain, they attempt a daring mission to sabotage the dirigible-like craft being transported by land vehicles. Mission fails when Sally, Bunnie and Antoine are captured.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: When Sonic and The Freedom Fighters take out Robotnik's main computer, they're trapped by Swat-Bots in an underground tunnel, only to be rescued by a handsome teenager named Griff. Griff takes them deep beneath the Big City.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Robotnik severely upsets the ecosystem above and below the surface, when he sends explosive, Robotnik snake probes drilling deep under ground in search of oil lakes.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Tails brings a new friend home to Knothole - a baby Terra-Pod who has strayed from the herd that is currently migrating. Sonic, Sally, and some of the other Freedom Fighters return the Baby-T to its mom and the rest of the herd.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Sonic reluctantly takes Tails with him to the Big City to find some cotter pins for the Freedom Fighters catapults. While in the City, Sonic is nearly eaten alive by his old dog Muttski, now a roboti-sized guard dog in the Junk Heaps

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: The Freedom Fighters, with new addition, Dulcy, develop a De-Roboti-sizer and try it out on Bunnie. It's a success and, against Sally's wishes, Sonic battles Robotnik's newest weapon, a Virtual Reality Shriek-Bot.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: The Freedom Fighters run into a Swashbuckler, named Ari, battling Swat-Bots. Sonic diverts them, allowing Ari to escape. After hearing that Robotnik is holding his band of Freedom Fighters hostage, Ari decides to help Sonic free them.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: The sinister Robotnik blackmails Cyrus, an old friend of Sonic's, by holding his father hostage.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: On a trip to Port Mobius, Sonic, Manic and Sonia are told that ships are sinking due to the Mobian sea-beast.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: Rumor has it that Queen Aleena's journals are buried in the city of Tashistan. Sonic and his siblings search the city for the scrolls.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: While lost in the wastelands of Mobius, Sonic, Manic and Sonia stumble upon an oasis called Lake Valley Resort.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: Sonia is invited to a debutante ball, but before she can be officially announced to society, she and her brothers, Manic and Sonic have an important mission.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: Robotnik organizes a track meet to lure Sonic into a trap.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: The Hedgehogs find out that Sonia has inherited a castle. She basks in the idea of having a castle for headquarters.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: In Rainbow Village, people are busy preparing for a dance marathon, a competition in which all of the villagers participate. Ling Ling is very sad that Ellie decided not to participate. Ellie is not a confident dancer so she doesn't want to participate. // Rainbow Ruby's friends tell her that they are now friends with Felicia. They were playing hide and seek but Felicia is such a good hider that she's refused to come out even though she's won the game.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Kiki wants to become a movie actress but the villagers have no idea how to direct a movie. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to turn into a movie director. She then assigns roles to the village friends. // Ling Ling rushes toward Rainbow Ruby to tell her that a busy villager mom asked him to take care of her three children but he just doesn't have time to look after them. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to transform into a babysitter and starts babysitting the children.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Princess Kiki is asleep, UP IN THE AIR! According to the note that Kiki left behind, a princess falls under a spell from time to time and in order to break it, they need to play the music box nearby Kiki. But the music box is broken. // Rainbow Ruby and Choco discover cars moving in every direction. Ling Ling asks Rainbow Ruby for help and she uses her magic suitcase to become a police officer.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that today is Noodle Festival day in the village but there is a problem. The problem is that the noodle tree, which grows noodles, decided to take a year off and is not growing any noodles this year. // Ling Ling and the friends are worried about the discouraged Gina. Gina just announced that she will stop doing somersaults anymore because she accidentally hurt her friends while practicing.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that Rainbow Village friends were playing around the Rainbow fountain to cool off from the heat but water suddenly stopped coming out of the fountain. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a plumber and starts fixing the fountain. // Kiki's face has turned purple because she ate magical Plumen berries. Kiki accidentally picked magical Plumen berries instead of the delicious rainbow berries she was trying to gather.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ellie's and Jessy's hair are a complete mess after Felicia accidentally causes some Rainbow fruit to fall on them. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a hairdresser. // It's winter in Rainbow Village. Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that last night a huge ice mountain appeared so everyone is enjoying an ice sculpture festival. But Jessy seems to have a toothache.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: The ground is all wet in Rainbow Village. What's going on? The friends tell Ruby that Jessy's tears have soaked the ground. Jessy accidentally ate Ellie's magic beans and became as huge as a giant. // Thunderbell is very sad because his best friend, Mr. Sloth, is angry at him. Rainbow Ruby thinks up the best way to apologize to Mr. Sloth, who loves performances. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a choreographer.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Thunderbell, Ellie, and Kiki are looking hard for something. It turns out that when Thunderbell was trying on Kiki's crown, he accidentally dropped it and a gem went missing. Kiki sadly says that a princess' crown is worthless without a jewel. // Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that this year they won't be able to enjoy the traditional rainbow dumplings as the recipe was accidentally torn to pieces. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a chef.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Kiki wanted to make a nice garden so she asked the Poppies to do some gardening but they planted the seeds everywhere in the garden, making an absolute mess. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to turn into a gardener.// Ling Ling is setting up a 'Thankfulness' tree at the center of the town square. He tells Rainbow Ruby that he is going to hold an event where the village friends can write notes to people that they are thankful to and hang them up on the tree.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Rainbow Village friends are on a train and they are concerned that the train won't stop. The engine is even smoking! Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a firefighter. // Paige is extremely upset. It turns out that she's been preparing an awesome fashion show but now it had to be cancelled because Gina accidentally spilled a bunch of fruit onto the stage.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Rainbow Village friends are gathered together at the lake to surf on the Rainbow wave that only comes once a year and they need some help. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a lifeguard and helps her friends safely enjoy their surfing. // Thunderbell is looking extremely worried. It turns out that his pet fish has gone missing. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a detective and finds out Thunderbell's pet fish's whereabouts.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: A welcome party for Prince Frederick is taking place in Rainbow Village, but the guest of honour isn't having any fun at all. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a party planner. // The village is a total mess from the paint that spilled from up above. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to turn into a clean-up engineer and starts cleaning with her friends.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: A Flower Festival is underway in Rainbow Village and Ling Ling has called Rainbow Ruby to help her judge the "best flower" contest. But just as Rainbow Ruby starts looking around at the flowers, Kiki's pet dog Pom Pom starts acting unusual and running around frantically, destroying the festival! // Gina and Jessy are with a huge fish that's injured by the lake. Rainbow Ruby becomes a Marine Biologist. Rainbow Ruby uses her encyclopedia to find out that the fish is actually a dolphin.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Everyone is frightened because some visiting birds keep trying to sit on their heads. According to Chirpee, the birds just enjoy sitting on people's heads. Rainbow Ruby designs hats where the birds can comfortably sit without disturbing the villagers. // The village friends were planning to have some fun with the rainbow fish but they're all disappointed to discover that the lake has frozen over.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: The friends are very tired because Mr. Moon hasn't come to sing them their nightly lullaby. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become an Astronaut and flies to space to look for Mr. Moon with her friends. // In Rainbow Village, a super frantic Thunderbell is high up in the air, trapped inside a giant bubble. Thunderbell got trapped in a giant bubble in the middle of a bubble bath. In order to find out a way to bring Thunderbell back to earth, Rainbow Ruby turns into a scientist.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Gina accidentally broke Felicia's house, leaving Felicia homeless. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become an architect and builds a house that suits Felicia's demands. // Gina is extremely worried. She needs strawberries for the Rainbow Fruit Ice Cream Festival that's starting soon but the strawberries are all mixed in with other red objects.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry Shortcake is planning a first birthday party for her sister Apple Dumpling, so she sets off on an adventure to find the perfect party supplies.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake

programme.title: Doolittle's Raiders: A Final Toast
programme.desc: The bottle of 1896 Hennessy Cognac was uncorked in front of hundreds of people. The surviving World War II veterans from one of historys greatest military missions were about to raise their silver goblets one last time ending a decades long tradition. It was time for the veterans to hold this final toast. They could wait no longer. Their numbers had dwindled to just a few. Many of the names of the 80 flyers who took part in the April 18, 1942 raid on Japan may not be familiar to most, but collectively they will always be known in history as the Doolittle Tokyo Raiders. Long ago these American flyers, aboard 16 B-25 bombers, had accomplished a daring mission that changed the morale of an entire nation. The aviators, led by famed commander Lt. Col. James Harold Doolittle, had taken the fight directly to the enemy for the first time in the war and delivered retribution for Japans sneak attack on Pearl Harbor on December 7, 1941.

programme.title: Eagles of Mercy
programme.desc: EAGLES OF MERCY recounts a seldom-told chapter in the World War II narrative, picking up in the opening moments of D-Day. In the early hours of June 6, 1944, two young American medics with the 101st Airborne Screaming Eagles parachuted into France, and soon found themselves trapped in a 12th-century Norman church in the small village of Angoville-au-Plain. Medics Robert Wright and Kenneth Moore provided first aid to the first casualties of D-Day while a savage battle raged outside between American and German forces. During the documentary, Wright and Moore recount the rigors of basic training, parachuting into Normandy ahead of the amphibious landing on Utah Beach, surviving German anti-aircraft artillery and carrying on their life-saving work even when their unit retreated. EAGLES OF MERCY supplements these compelling stories with newsreel footage, re-enactments and interviews with fellow medics and residents of Angoville.

programme.title: Navy Heroes of Normandy
programme.desc: On June 6, 1944, more than 52,000 American sailors on board thousands of U.S. Navy ships arrived off a quiet stretch of coast in Normandy, France, setting in motion he largest amphibious and landing assault of World War II. Their mission, Operation Neptune-Overlord, would prove critical to the success of the Allied invasion of Western Europe. Despite the casualties of D-Day 1,068 Americans died in the initial assault on Hitler's fortressed coastline no monument stood to honor their sacrifices. NAVY HEROES OF NORMANDY chronicles the building and dedication of the first-ever U.S. Navy monument in Normandy. In an emotional climax, a large gathering of dignitaries, French citizens and D-Day veterans witness the installation and dedication of the 12-foot-tall bronze sculpture on France's Utah Beach, a long-overdue official recognition for the crucial role they played on that fateful day.

programme.title: Omaha Beach: Honor and Sacrifice
programme.desc: OMAHA BEACH: HONOR AND SACRIFICE shows the very personal stories of several veterans as they return to Omaha Beach and documents the celebration in Normandy that continues to this day as a result of their acts of courage and determination on June 6, 1944.On D-Day, a roughly 7,000-yard stretch of beach in Normandy, France given the code name Omaha proved to be the Allies' biggest obstacle to the success of Operation Overlord. The assignment to take Omaha Beach, establish a beachhead, and move inland into France was given to two American divisions the already battle-hardened 1st Infantry Division (The Big Red One) and the untested 29th Infantry Division (The Blue and the Gray), who had yet to see any combat in World War II.American Naval Combat Demolition Units also hit the beach that day, writing their own individual stories of horror and heroism. Seven decades after the Boys of Omaha Beach landed, many veterans are returning to this part of the Normandy coast for the last time. Other soldiers who were there on D-Day have also come back for the first time since that historic day, looking for closure as they enter the final years of their lives.

programme.title: Behind the Wings
programme.desc: A continued exploration of the Artemis missions, building on Episode 404 of Behind the Wings. Look back at Apollo to understand what we learned from going to the moon for the first time. Follow the progress of the program, from the development of new lunar architecture to the training of the astronauts who will take the next steps on the moon. In a Virtual Reality lab at Lockheed Martin Space, Engineers discuss the design of the Orion spacecraft. At Kennedy Space Center, technicians reflect on the learnings from Artemis I. At NASA's Johnson Space Center, astronauts are training for Artemis II, the first Artemis crewed mission. Meet the diverse team working on the program and discover how returning to the Moon is a crucial step towards new scientific discoveries, a cis-lunar economy, and inspiring the next generation of explorers.

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Why is there a giant church complex, the DomQuartier, in this tiny Alpine town? And why is there an equally giant medieval fortress high above it? Who lived in Mirabell Palace? And Hellbrun Palace? Why is Getreidegassse so charming, and why are those shop signs so important? And how do you solve a problem like Maria? From the Sound of Music to Silent Night to the Sonatas of this towns beloved Mozart, its time to get curious about Salzburg.

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: How did this UNESCO town in Mexico get such a curious name? And what do a duck and a dog have to do with it? Why are there so many grand mansions in such a small town? Why do postcards have to do with its soaring Gothic church? Why is there an art school in a former convent? And how did San Miguel de Allende become a magnet for retired American military on the G.I. Bill?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Why did the Scottish kings live in Edinburgh, and why dont we have Scottish kings anymore? Why was Edinburgh Castle built high upon on Castle Rock? Whats the connection between Edinburgh and Harry Potter? And where are the real-life inspirations for Harry Potter locations? What is so royal about Edinburghs Royal Mile? Who is Arthur, and why does he get his own Seat?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Why is there a stone giant holding a flame, high in the hills overlooking the town? How did silver help shape this Mexican town? Why are its streets and alleys so winding, and what happened to the Guanajuato River? What does the towns main church have to do with the King of Spain? Why does the towns marketplace look like a Paris train station? And its theatre look like the Paris Opera House?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Why is a European Christmas Market also called Weihnachtsmarkt or a Christkindlmarkt? And what do these markets have to do with the Holy Roman Empire? Why do you find Christmas Markets mostly in German-speaking countries? What is the curious history of the Advent Wreath, of Silent Night, and a creepy Christmas character named Krampus? And is a Krampus different than a Krapfen?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: You may not be able to live like a royal, but wouldnt it be nice to shop like one? In this episode, we explore the curious world of Royal Warrants, a special designation given to only the best shops and services, literally fit for a king (or queen, or prince or princess). Kind of like knighthood for a shop. From the royal dressmakers to tiara makers to the royal cheesemonger (yes, there is such a thing), we take you across London, inside the world of the royals.
